chapter_goal: Use the first fundamental form as a computational measuring device for length, angle, area, and spherical geometry.

visual_sequence.inspection_target: changing G changes allowable coordinate velocity and local area density
visual_sequence.representation: unit-speed ellipses, coefficient curves, and area density comparison
visual_sequence.validation: positive determinant and sampled metric table

visual_sequence.inspection_target: the curve bends in space but the metric length is unchanged
visual_sequence.representation: same parameter curve in a plane strip and wrapped cylinder

visual_sequence.inspection_target: angles survive while scale varies with radius
visual_sequence.representation: stereographic conformal scale field and local angle test

visual_sequence.inspection_target: sphere and cylinder area densities both equal cos(theta)
visual_sequence.representation: sphere band and matching cylinder band
visual_sequence.validation: determinants of the pulled-back metrics agree

visual_sequence.inspection_target: side lengths are central angles and area is angular excess
visual_sequence.representation: great-circle triangle plus stereographic distance sample
visual_sequence.validation: cosine rule, sine rule, excess, and stereographic distance residuals
